ruleset.name: CSharp Rules
ruleset.description:
PHPMD's default rules tuned for idiomatic C#. It pulls in every built-in ruleset, but quiets a few rules whose PHP defaults are a poor fit for C#:
* Naming/ShortVariable is dropped: short names (x, e, i, kv, ...) are idiomatic in lambdas, event handlers, and tight scopes. * Naming/LongVariable's maximum is raised to 35, since C# favors descriptive PascalCase/camelCase names over PHP's terser style. * CleanCode/ElseExpression is dropped: `else` is unobjectionable C#; no mainstream C# analyzer flags it. * CleanCode/BooleanArgumentFlag is dropped: boolean parameters are common throughout the BCL (StringComparison aside, e.g. Stream, XmlWriter). * CleanCode/StaticAccess is dropped: static access is pervasive and idiomatic in C# (Math, string.Join, Path, File, Convert, ...). * UnusedCode/UnusedFormalParameter is dropped: unused parameters are routinely required by interface implementations, event-handler signatures (object sender, EventArgs e), and delegate shapes. * Design/CountInLoopExpression is dropped: Count/Length properties are O(1) in .NET collections, unlike PHP's count().
These dropped rules are bundled in the opt-in `opinionated` ruleset for anyone who wants the stricter, more PHP-flavoured style. See opinionated.xml.
